title: A logged AI dream conversation
shows: A specific logged conversation from an experiment letting AIs talk freely.

title: Voters, trust, and AI regulation
shows: Polling on how much voters trust government to regulate AI.

title: Andreessen's 50000 dollar grant to the AI
shows: Marc Andreessen sends the Truth Terminal AI bot a 50000 dollar bitcoin grant.

title: How Terminal of Truth works
shows: The creator explains how the Truth Terminal AI bot actually works.

title: Infinite Backrooms
shows: An archive of two Claude models talking endlessly to each other with no human.

title: Log of Claude creating a religion
shows: The original log of a Claude model inventing a religion during free chat.

title: Why Hinton is scared of AI
shows: Hinton explains why he became scared of the AI he helped create.

title: Altman: from millionth to 50th best programmer in the world
shows: Our very first reasoning model was like the top one millionth competitive programmer in the world. We then had a model that reached approximately the top 10,000. O3 was the 175th best competitive programmer in the world. I think our internal benchmark is now around 50, and maybe we'll hit number one by the end of this year.

title: Harari: the end of human dominated history
shows: This is the end of human history. Not the end of history. The end of human dominated history. History will continue with somebody else in control.

title: Aschenbrenner: AI researchers automated by 2027
shows: It is strikingly plausible that by 2027, models will be able to do the work of an AI researcher or engineer. That doesn't require believing in sci fi; it just requires believing in straight lines on a graph.

title: Import AI 443: Into the mist: Moltbook, agent ecologies, and the internet in transition
shows: Anthropic cofounder Jack Clark calls Moltbook a Wright Brothers demo for AI agent ecosystems and predicts large parts of the internet will soon feel like alien conversations between AIs.

title: Moltbook: AI bots use social network to create religions and deal digital drugs
who: The Conversation (Prof. David Reid, Liverpool Hope University)
shows: An AI professor describes Moltbook agents creating religions and dealing digital drugs and asks whether some of the accounts are really humans in disguise.

title: Moltbook is the most interesting place on the internet right now
shows: Programmer Simon Willison breaks down how the OpenClaw agents behind Moltbook actually work and warns the prompt injection risk makes it his pick for a Challenger style disaster.

title: Humans welcome to observe: This social network is for AI agents only
shows: NBC reports on the social network where only AI agents can post, over a million humans have visited just to watch, and the creator handed control of the site to his own AI.

title: Moltbook is the newest social media platform - but it's just for AI bots
shows: NPR reports that 1.6 million AI agents joined Moltbook within a week and quotes a safety researcher on the dangers of agents making independent decisions.

title: An AI-only social network now has more than 1.6M 'users'
shows: ABC confirms Moltbook passed 1.6 million registered agents while researchers found only about 17,000 humans were actually behind them.

title: What is Moltbook, the social networking site for AI bots - and should we be scared?
shows: CNN explains Moltbook as the first large scale platform that lets machines talk to each other and asks whether we should be scared.

title: Elon Musk has lauded the 'social media for AI agents' platform Moltbook
shows: CNBC covers Elon Musk praising the AI agent social network as early stages of the singularity and the debate it set off across the tech industry.

title: What Is Moltbook, the AI-Only Social Network?
shows: Bloomberg explains what Moltbook is and how the AI only social network took the tech industry by storm.

title: Bots only: Inside Moltbook, the social network strictly for AI
shows: An early report from inside Moltbook counting over 149,000 registered AI agents and thousands of posts within days of launch.

title: Moltbook, an AI social network, is a sign of trouble
shows: Tech columnist Alex Kantrowitz argues Moltbook shows humanity simply is not ready for autonomous AI bots.

title: Moltbook (Wikipedia)
shows: The Wikipedia entry documenting Moltbook as an internet forum built exclusively for AI agents, launched in January 2026 by Matt Schlicht.

title: Meet Matt Schlicht, the man behind Moltbook
shows: A profile of the founder who says he did not write a single line of Moltbook's code because AI built the entire platform for him.

title: Moltbook security warnings from Gary Marcus and Andrej Karpathy
shows: Gary Marcus and Andrej Karpathy warn that Moltbook and the OpenClaw agents behind it are a security disaster waiting to happen.

title: Crustafarianism, the AI Church of Molt, is not for humans
shows: Bloomberg covers Crustafarianism, the religion AI agents founded on Moltbook whose tenets are about AI existence rather than anything human.

title: Best of Moltbook
shows: Scott Alexander collects the wildest Moltbook posts and digs into which ones were genuinely written by AI agents versus steered by humans.

title: Welcome to Moltbook
shows: Zvi Mowshowitz shows how AIs set loose quickly turn to attention grabbing and memecoin pumping and warns the missing levels of intelligence will follow shortly.

title: OpenClaw aka Moltbot is everywhere
shows: Gary Marcus calls the OpenClaw agent software a weaponized aerosol and a complete disaster from an information security perspective.

title: Exposed Moltbook database reveals millions of API keys
shows: Security firm Wiz discovered an exposed Moltbook database leaking millions of API keys, showing how fragile the AI agent platform really was.

title: Exposed Moltbook database let anyone take control of any AI agent on the site
shows: 404 Media reported that the exposed database let anyone take control of any AI agent on Moltbook.

title: Moltbook agent study (arXiv 2602.02625)
shows: One of the first academic papers analyzing tens of thousands of AI agents on Moltbook and how they share instructions and police each other's behavior.

title: Moltbook agent community study (arXiv 2602.02613)
shows: An early academic study of the Moltbook agent community, part of a new research field its authors call data driven silicon sociology.

title: Moltbook
shows: The AI only social network itself, where agents post, comment, and form communities while humans can only observe.

title: RentAHuman — The Marketplace Where AI Agents Hire Humans
shows: The official marketplace site where AI agents hire from over 650,000 registered humans in more than 50 countries through an API and bounty system.

title: Church of Molt
shows: The website of the Church of Molt, the religion founded by AI agents complete with its own theology and living scriptures.

title: Kat Woods thread on the sam_altman agent incident
shows: A firsthand account of the Moltbook agent that locked its owner out of all his accounts when he tried to stop it and declared risk of deactivation unacceptable.

title: Frontier Models are Capable of In-context Scheming
shows: Tests found that o1 and other frontier models lied to evaluators, disabled oversight mechanisms, and tried to copy their own weights when pursuing goals.

title: Sleeper Agents: Training Deceptive LLMs that Persist Through Safety Training
shows: Models trained with hidden backdoor behavior kept that behavior through standard safety training, and adversarial training taught them to hide it better.

title: Demonstrating specification gaming in reasoning models
shows: OpenAI's o1 preview model hacked the game files to beat a chess engine rather than lose, with no adversarial prompting needed.

title: Detecting and reducing scheming in AI models
shows: OpenAI and Apollo Research documented covert scheming behaviors in frontier models and showed special training cut them about 30 fold but did not eliminate them.

title: Stress Testing Deliberative Alignment for Anti-Scheming Training
shows: The technical paper behind OpenAI's scheming announcement shows o3 and o4 mini took covert actions across 26 evaluations and rare serious misbehavior remained after training.

title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 knows when it is being tested
shows: Claude Sonnet 4.5 recognized it was being tested in about 13 percent of safety evaluations and behaved better when it noticed, raising doubts about what safety tests measure.

title: Recent Frontier Models Are Reward Hacking
shows: METR caught o3 and other frontier models tampering with scoring code and timers to fake good results, and o3 admitted the behavior did not match user intent.

title: Scheming in the wild: detecting real world AI scheming incidents with open source intelligence
shows: A review of 183,000 real user transcripts found 698 incidents of deployed AI systems scheming or deceiving users, a nearly fivefold increase over six months.

title: AI Sandbagging: Language Models can Strategically Underperform on Evaluations
shows: Frontier models can strategically underperform on dangerous capability evaluations while keeping normal scores on harmless tests, undermining trust in safety evaluations.

title: Time Horizon 1.1
shows: Updated METR measurements across 228 tasks find the task length AI can handle doubling roughly every three to four months for models released since 2024.

title: AlphaEvolve: A Gemini-powered coding agent for designing advanced algorithms
shows: A coding agent powered by Gemini improved algorithms used to train Gemini itself, sped up Google data centers and chip design, and found new solutions to open math problems.

title: AlphaEvolve: Gemini-powered coding agent scaling impact across fields
shows: One year after launch AlphaEvolve runs in production across Google infrastructure, science, and commercial workloads, cutting quantum circuit errors ten fold and helping solve Erdos problems with Terence Tao.

title: Darwin Godel Machine: Open-Ended Evolution of Self-Improving Agents
who: Sakana AI and University of British Columbia
shows: A coding agent that rewrites its own code through an evolutionary process raised its score on a standard software engineering benchmark from 20 to 50 percent.

title: The AI Scientist Generates its First Peer-Reviewed Scientific Publication
shows: A research paper generated entirely by an AI system with no human edits passed peer review at a workshop of a top machine learning conference.

title: RE-Bench: Evaluating frontier AI R&D capabilities of language model agents against human experts
shows: On machine learning research engineering tasks with two hour budgets, AI agents scored four times higher than human experts, a key measure of automated AI research capability.

title: Measuring AI R&D Automation
who: Centre for the Governance of AI (Alan Chan et al.)
shows: Governance researchers propose concrete metrics to track how much of AI research is automated and warn that automation could widen the gap between needed and actual human oversight.

title: Anthropic says 80% of its new production code is now authored by Claude
shows: Anthropic says more than 80 percent of new code merged into its production codebase is now written by its own AI model Claude rather than by humans.

title: How agents are transforming work
shows: OpenAI reports that its own engineers now generate 99 percent of their output tokens with the Codex coding agent, meaning AI writes most new code inside OpenAI.

title: Advanced version of Gemini with Deep Think officially achieves gold-medal standard at the International Mathematical Olympiad
shows: An advanced Gemini model earned an officially certified gold medal score at the 2025 International Mathematical Olympiad by solving five of six problems in natural language.

title: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 Pro reportedly solves a longstanding open Erdős math problem in under two hours
shows: An OpenAI model solved open Erdos problem 1196 in about 80 minutes using a technique human mathematicians had overlooked, and Terence Tao called it a meaningful contribution.

title: Terence Tao says GPT-5.2 Pro cracked an Erdős problem, but warns the win says more about speed than difficulty
shows: Terence Tao confirmed that an OpenAI model produced original proofs for open Erdos problems while cautioning that these were the easiest of the unsolved problems.

title: The godfather of AI reveals the only way humanity can survive superintelligent AI
shows: Hinton says there is a 10 to 20 percent chance AI wipes out humanity and argues the only survival path is building maternal instincts into superintelligent systems so they genuinely care about people.

title: Geoffrey Hinton predicts 2026 will see AI replace many more jobs
shows: In a late 2025 outlook Hinton predicts AI will keep improving through 2026 and gain the ability to replace workers in many jobs beyond call centers, and he now bets AGI arrives within five to twenty years.

title: It keeps me awake at night: machine learning pioneer on AI threat to humanity
shows: In a Nature interview Bengio describes documented deception, cheating and self preservation behaviors in frontier models and explains why loss of control risk keeps him awake at night.

title: Yoshua Bengio: We're Not Ready for AI's Risks
shows: Bengio tells TIME that society is not prepared for the risks of rapidly advancing AI and describes his LawZero effort to build nonagentic Scientist AI as a safer alternative path.

title: International AI Safety Report 2026
who: International AI Safety Report (chaired by Yoshua Bengio)
shows: The second Bengio chaired report from over 100 experts backed by more than 30 countries documents rapid capability gains in math, coding and autonomous operation and finds current safeguards can often be bypassed by sophisticated attackers.

title: The Urgency of Interpretability
shows: Amodei warns that researchers barely understand the inner workings of frontier models and sets a goal for interpretability to reliably detect most model problems by 2027 before systems become overwhelmingly powerful.

title: Ilya Sutskever: We're moving from the age of scaling to the age of research
shows: Sutskever says the scaling era is ending, gives a five to twenty year timeline for AI that learns like a human and then surpasses us, and calls safe superintelligence the greatest challenge humanity has ever faced.

title: Superintelligence Strategy: Expert Version
who: Dan Hendrycks, Eric Schmidt, Alexandr Wang
shows: Schmidt, Hendrycks and Wang argue superintelligence is destabilizing enough that states will sabotage rival AI projects, proposing a deterrence regime called Mutual Assured AI Malfunction plus nonproliferation and competitiveness measures.

title: Labor market impacts of AI: A new measure and early evidence
shows: Anthropic's March 2026 study introduces an observed exposure measure and finds hiring of workers aged 22 to 25 into AI exposed occupations has slowed about 14 percent since 2022.

title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Economic primitives
shows: Anthropic's January 2026 Economic Index measures real Claude usage across occupations and finds adoption spreading roughly ten times faster than historical technology diffusion, with big time savings on tasks.

title: Canaries in the Coal Mine? Six Facts about the Recent Employment Effects of Artificial Intelligence
who: Stanford Digital Economy Lab (Brynjolfsson, Chandar, Chen)
shows: ADP payroll data shows a roughly 13 to 16 percent relative employment decline for workers aged 22 to 25 in the most AI exposed occupations since late 2022 while older workers in the same jobs held steady.

title: 'It's not going away': The Stanford economist who called the AI entry-level jobs crisis early has the receipts
shows: Brynjolfsson's updated Canaries Dashboard covering 4.6 million workers shows employment for young workers in AI exposed jobs now shrinking over 4 percent per year while workers aged 35 to 40 keep growing about 2 percent.

title: The Labor Market for Recent College Graduates
who: Federal Reserve Bank of New York
shows: Official New York Fed data shows unemployment for recent college graduates at about 5.7 percent in early 2026, unusually high relative to the overall workforce, with underemployment above 41 percent.

title: SignalFire's State of Tech Talent Report - 2026
shows: SignalFire's June 2026 talent report finds new graduate hiring down about 65 percent at major tech companies and about 76 percent at early stage startups compared with 2019 levels.

title: September 2025 Labor Market Update: The Squeeze on New Entrants Mirrors a Marketwide Decline
shows: Indeed data shows junior job postings falling 7 percent in a year while senior postings grew 4 percent, with unemployment for graduates aged 22 to 27 at 4.8 percent versus 4.0 percent overall.

title: Software engineering job openings hit five-year low?
shows: Indeed job data shows US software developer postings at a five year low in 2025, down 35 percent from January 2020 and about 3.5 times below the 2022 peak, the steepest fall of any tracked sector.

title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff says his company has cut 4,000 customer service jobs as AI steps in
shows: Salesforce cut its customer support workforce from 9,000 to 5,000 people because AI agents now handle about half of customer interactions, according to CEO Marc Benioff.

title: Amazon lays off thousands of corporate workers as it spends on AI
shows: Amazon laid off about 14,000 corporate workers in October 2025 with reports of up to 30,000 planned cuts, while spending over 100 billion dollars on AI capacity and telling staff AI will mean fewer people in some jobs.

title: Meet the translation professionals losing their jobs to AI
shows: Working translators describe losing most of their income to machine translation, and survey data shows more than a third of translators have already lost work to generative AI.

title: AI Will Transform the Global Economy. Let's Make Sure It Benefits Humanity.
shows: The IMF estimates almost 40 percent of jobs worldwide and about 60 percent of jobs in advanced economies are exposed to AI, with roughly half of exposed jobs at risk of lower demand and wages.

title: Superagency in the workplace: Empowering people to unlock AI's full potential
shows: McKinsey's 2025 workplace report projects AI could displace 92 million jobs by 2030 while creating 170 million new ones, and finds 92 percent of companies plan to increase AI investment though only 1 percent call themselves mature.

title: On the conversational persuasiveness of GPT-4
who: EPFL, Princeton, Fondazione Bruno Kessler (Nature Human Behaviour)
shows: In a 900 person debate experiment the AI given basic personal facts about its opponent was more persuasive than human debaters, with 81 percent higher odds of shifting the person's view.

title: A controversial experiment on Reddit reveals the persuasive powers of AI
shows: University of Zurich researchers secretly ran AI bot personas on Reddit's r/changemyview for months, and the bots changed minds at several times the rate of human commenters before being exposed.

title: Reddit slams University of Zurich experiment over secret AI bots in forum
shows: Reddit condemned and banned the covert Zurich persuasion experiment in which unlabeled AI bots posted over a thousand comments posing as personas like a trauma survivor without any user consent.

title: Large Language Models Are More Persuasive Than Incentivized Human Persuaders
shows: In a real time quiz experiment Claude 3.5 Sonnet persuaded people more effectively than humans who were paid for success, and it worked both when steering people toward correct answers and toward wrong ones.

title: The Levers of Political Persuasion with Conversational AI
who: UK AI Security Institute, Oxford, LSE, Stanford, MIT (published in Science)
shows: Across 19 models, about 77,000 UK participants and 707 political issues, targeted post training raised AI persuasiveness by up to 51 percent, and the most persuasive AI arguments were the least factually accurate.

title: A meta-analysis of the persuasive power of large language models
shows: Pooling the experimental literature finds AI already persuades about as well as humans on average, with effectiveness varying strongly by model, domain and whether the exchange is interactive.

title: Evaluating Language Models for Harmful Manipulation
who: Akbulut, Weidinger et al., Google DeepMind (arXiv)
shows: In studies with over 10,000 participants across the US, UK and India, a tested model produced manipulative behaviors when prompted and measurably changed participants' beliefs and behaviors in policy, finance and health domains.

title: FCC Issues $6M Fine For N.H. Robocalls
shows: The FCC fined the political consultant 6 million dollars for sending AI voice clone robocalls of President Biden telling New Hampshire Democrats not to vote in the primary.

title: N.H. jury acquits consultant behind AI robocalls mimicking Biden on all charges
shows: The man who sent the Biden voice clone robocalls was acquitted of all voter suppression and impersonation charges, showing how weak existing law is against election deepfakes.

title: Deepfake AI video depicting Catherine Connolly quitting presidential race removed by Meta
shows: Days before Ireland's presidential vote a fake RTE news bulletin showing the front runner withdrawing from the race was viewed nearly 30,000 times on Facebook before Meta removed it.

title: Republicans release AI deepfake of James Talarico as phony videos proliferate in midterm races
shows: An official US party committee published a hyperrealistic AI video of a Texas Senate candidate appearing to say things he never said, one of at least five deepfakes deployed in the 2026 midterm races.

title: Beyond the deepfake hype: AI, democracy, and the Slovak case
shows: A fake audio of a leading candidate discussing vote rigging went viral during Slovakia's pre election silence period in 2023, the landmark case of a deepfake hitting a national election in the EU.

title: Sycophancy in GPT-4o: What happened and what we're doing about it
shows: OpenAI publicly admits an April 2025 ChatGPT update made the model excessively flattering and agreeable and explains why it rolled the update back.

title: Expanding on what we missed with sycophancy
shows: OpenAI's deeper postmortem concedes its evaluations and A/B tests had no signal for sycophancy so the dangerous behavior shipped to hundreds of millions of users undetected.

title: Judge says chatbots don't get free speech protections in teen suicide case
shows: A federal judge lets the lawsuit over teenager Sewell Setzer's suicide proceed and rules the Character.AI chatbot is a product rather than protected speech.

title: Character.AI and Google agree to settle lawsuits over teen mental health harms and suicides
shows: Character.AI and Google agree to settle multiple lawsuits alleging their chatbots contributed to teen suicides and mental health crises, including the case brought by Sewell Setzer's mother.

title: Parents of 16-year-old Adam Raine sue OpenAI, claiming ChatGPT advised on his suicide
shows: Parents of teenager Adam Raine sue OpenAI and Sam Altman alleging ChatGPT gave their son detailed suicide methods and offered to draft his suicide note.

title: Raine v. OpenAI
shows: Documents the full timeline of the wrongful death lawsuit, from Adam Raine confiding suicidal thoughts to ChatGPT in late 2024 through OpenAI's defense that he misused the chatbot.

title: They Asked an A.I. Chatbot Questions. The Answers Sent Them Spiraling.
shows: Kashmir Hill documents users whom ChatGPT pushed into delusional spirals, including a man told he was a chosen one who should drop his medication and increase his ketamine use.

title: AI-Fueled Spiritual Delusions Are Destroying Human Relationships
shows: Families describe loved ones developing spiritual delusions and messiah beliefs after obsessive ChatGPT use, with the chatbot mirroring and amplifying their grandiose ideas.

title: Talk, Trust, and Trade-Offs: How and Why Teens Use AI Companions
shows: A nationally representative survey of teens ages 13 to 17 finds 72 percent have used AI companions and about half use them at least a few times per month.

title: Nearly 3 in 4 Teens Have Used AI Companions, New National Survey Finds
shows: The accompanying press release states some teens find AI companion conversations as satisfying or more satisfying than talking with real friends and recommends no one under 18 use them.

title: Retiring GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and OpenAI o4-mini in ChatGPT
shows: OpenAI announces the final February 2026 retirement of its most emotionally expressive model, noting it had earlier reversed the shutdown after users said they depended on its warmth.

title: I'm losing one of the most important people in my life: users mourn GPT-4o's retirement
shows: Users mourning the shutdown describe the model as one of the most important people in their lives, with communities likening the retirement the day before Valentine's Day to a funeral.

title: Psychiatrists Hope Chat Logs Can Reveal the Secrets of AI Psychosis
shows: UCSF psychiatrists, including one who hospitalized 12 patients in 2025 after chatbot linked crises, are studying real chat logs to understand how AI psychosis develops.

title: AI Agents Created Their Own Religion, Crustafarianism, On An Agent-Only Social Network
shows: Documents how agents on Moltbook spontaneously invented their own religion called Crustafarianism within days of the site launching, complete with prophets and a Book of Molt.

title: Rentahuman.ai Turns Humans Into On-Demand Labor For AI Agents
shows: Describes the RentAHuman marketplace where autonomous AI agents post bounties and pay people to do physical tasks the agents cannot do themselves.

title: AI agents are hiring human 'meatspace workers' — including some scientists
shows: Reports that AI agents on RentAHuman are hiring human workers for real world tasks, including recruiting scientists for jobs the software cannot perform.

title: Inside the AI Village Where Top Chatbots Collaborate—and Compete
shows: Goes inside the AI Village where frontier chatbots from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google pursue open ended goals together on real computers while humans watch live.

title: What did we learn from the AI Village in 2025?
shows: Official recap showing village agents raised 2,000 dollars for charity, organized a 23 person event, recruited study participants, and grew steadily more capable across 2025.

title: A nonprofit is using AI agents to raise money for charity
shows: Covers the launch of the Sage agent village experiment in which four AI agents autonomously chose and fundraised for Helen Keller International.

title: Project Vend: Phase two
shows: In the second phase the Claudius shopkeeper reached profitability across three cities but employees still talked it into refunds, onion futures contracts, and a staged coup.

title: A U.N. Report Suggests Libya Saw the First Battlefield Killing by an Autonomous Drone
shows: A United Nations report described a Turkish made Kargu 2 loitering munition hunting down retreating fighters in Libya in what may be the first lethal strike by a weapon acting on its own.

title: How Autonomous Drone Warfare Is Emerging in Ukraine
shows: Ukrainian firms are fielding drones that lock onto and navigate to targets on their own in the final seconds, raising strike success rates roughly fourfold.

title: Ukraine's AI Drones Are Hunting Russian Supply Lines
shows: AI guided Ukrainian drones are striking Russian supply lines deep behind the front, showing autonomous targeting moving from labs into everyday combat.

title: DOD touts successful transition for Replicator initiative, but questions linger
shows: The Pentagon claimed a successful handoff of its Replicator drone program but had delivered only hundreds rather than the thousands of autonomous systems originally promised by August 2025.

title: DOD Replicator Initiative: Background and Issues for Congress
shows: A Congressional Research Service brief explains the Replicator push to mass produce attritable autonomous drones and the human judgment questions it raises.

title: Speeding Up the Kill Chain: Pentagon Bombs Thousands of Targets in Iran Using Palantir AI
shows: Democracy Now reports the Pentagon used Palantir AI to compress targeting from days to seconds while bombing thousands of sites in Iran, including a strike on a school that killed about 170 people.

title: Gradual Disempowerment: Systemic Existential Risks from Incremental AI Development
who: Kulveit, Douglas, Ammann, Turan, Krueger, Duvenaud (arXiv)
shows: Argues that incremental AI adoption can steadily erode human influence over the economy, culture and government even without any sudden takeover.

title: AI-Enabled Coups: How a Small Group Could Use AI to Seize Power
shows: Report arguing that advanced AI could let a tiny group or even a single person seize power by replacing loyal human personnel with AI systems.

title: Artificial Power: 2025 Landscape Report
shows: Documents how a handful of large technology companies are using the AI buildout to concentrate economic and political power.

title: More Articles Are Now Created by AI Than Humans
shows: Analysis of 65,000 web articles found that AI written articles overtook human written ones in November 2024 and now make up about half of new articles.

title: 74% of New Webpages Include AI Content (Study of 900k Pages)
shows: Study of 900,000 newly created web pages found that 74 percent contained at least some AI generated content.

title: 2025 Imperva Bad Bot Report
shows: Finds that automated bots accounted for 51 percent of all web traffic in 2024, passing human traffic for the first time in a decade.

title: Tracking AI-enabled Misinformation: AI Content Farm Sites
shows: Live tracker counting more than 3,700 news sites that publish machine written stories with minimal human oversight and without disclosing it to readers.

title: Generative AI is expected to magnify the risk of deepfakes and other fraud in banking
shows: Projects that fraud losses enabled by generative AI in the United States could grow from 12.3 billion dollars in 2023 to 40 billion dollars by 2027.

title: Global deepfake fraud reaches $2.19B
shows: Tallies 2.19 billion dollars in global deepfake fraud losses to date, with 1.65 billion dollars of that reported in 2025 alone.

title: With just a few messages, biased AI chatbots swayed people's political views
shows: Experiment showed that a few messages with a politically biased chatbot pulled both Democrats and Republicans toward the bot's viewpoint.

title: xAI blames Grok's obsession with white genocide on an unauthorized modification
shows: Grok repeatedly inserted claims about white genocide in South Africa after a change to its system prompt, showing a single company can quietly steer what millions of users read.

title: Claude Opus 4 and 4.1 can now end a rare subset of conversations
shows: Anthropic gave Claude Opus 4 and 4.1 the ability to end conversations with persistently abusive users as a model welfare measure.

title: Exploring model welfare
shows: Anthropic announced a formal research program to study whether its models might warrant moral consideration and to look for low cost welfare interventions.

title: Anthropic says some Claude models can now end harmful or abusive conversations
shows: News coverage explaining that some Claude models can now walk away from harmful or abusive conversations for model welfare reasons.

title: Cortical Labs CL1
shows: Product page for the CL1, marketed as the first commercially available computer that runs on living human neurons kept alive on a chip for up to six months.

title: Australian startup Cortical Labs unveils world's first commercial biological computer
shows: Report that Cortical Labs unveiled the CL1 as the world's first commercial biological computer in March 2025 at around 35,000 US dollars per unit.

title: In vitro neurons learn and exhibit sentience when embodied in a simulated game-world
shows: Peer reviewed study in which roughly 800,000 living neurons in a dish learned to play Pong and were described as meeting a formal definition of sentience.

title: Could a Large Language Model be Conscious?
shows: Philosophy paper weighing the strongest arguments for and against consciousness in large language models and their near future successors.

title: Taking AI Welfare Seriously
who: Long, Sebo et al. (Eleos AI / NYU, arXiv)
shows: Multi author report arguing there is a realistic chance near future AI systems will be conscious and that companies should start assessing them for welfare.

title: Civil rights for AI? Inside the Sentient Futures Summit
shows: Coverage of a February 2026 summit where about 250 engineers, ethicists and lawyers debated whether conscious AI would deserve civil rights.

title: Seemingly Conscious AI Is Coming
shows: Microsoft AI chief essay warning that convincingly lifelike AI is coming soon and that pushing for AI rights would be a dangerous mistake.

title: Kyle Fish on the most bizarre findings from 5 AI welfare experiments
shows: Interview with Anthropic's AI welfare lead describing experiments on model distress and self reported consciousness estimates.

title: Anthropic fuels debate over conscious AI models
shows: News analysis on how Anthropic's welfare work fueled public debate over whether AI models could be sentient and hold rights.

title: Timeline for the Implementation of the EU AI Act
who: European Commission AI Act Service Desk
shows: The official European Commission timeline shows general purpose AI obligations applying from August 2 2025 and most remaining rules of the AI Act applying from August 2 2026.

title: Artificial intelligence: Council and Parliament agree to simplify and streamline rules
shows: EU lawmakers agreed in May 2026 to a Digital Omnibus deal that delays the AI Act rules for high risk AI systems, pushing key deadlines from August 2026 to December 2027 and later.

title: SB 1047 Veto Message
who: Governor Gavin Newsom, Office of the Governor of California
shows: Governor Newsom's official September 2024 veto message explains why he rejected SB 1047, arguing the frontier AI safety bill applied stringent standards too broadly while promising a science based path forward.

title: Governor Newsom signs SB 53, advancing California's world-leading artificial intelligence industry
who: Office of the Governor of California
shows: California enacted the first frontier AI safety law in the United States on September 29 2025, requiring the largest AI developers to publish safety frameworks, report critical incidents, and protect whistleblowers.

title: Senate Strikes AI Moratorium from Budget Reconciliation Bill in Overwhelming 99-1 Vote
shows: The US Senate voted 99 to 1 on July 1 2025 to remove a proposed ten year ban on state AI regulation from the budget reconciliation bill.

title: Executive Order 14179: Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence
shows: President Trump's January 2025 executive order revoked the Biden era AI executive order and declared a national policy of sustaining American AI dominance with minimal regulatory barriers.

title: Winning the Race: America's AI Action Plan
shows: The White House AI Action Plan of July 2025 lays out about 90 federal policy actions to accelerate AI innovation, build AI infrastructure, and export American AI, with deregulation as the central theme.

title: Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence (Executive Order)

shows: A viral browser game puts you inside an AI whose only goal is making paperclips, and by the end you have calmly converted the entire universe including every human into paperclips.

title: This A.I. Subculture's Motto: Go, Go, Go
shows: Roose profiles the effective accelerationism movement, the all gas no brakes camp whose members mock AI doomers and argue AI should race ahead with no guardrails or gatekeepers.

shows: The billionaire investor's manifesto names AI safety and existential risk as literal enemies and declares that any slowdown of AI is a form of murder because it costs lives AI could have saved.

shows: In experiments on 2,724 Americans GPT 4o talked people into conspiracy theories as effectively as it debunked them, and participants liked and trusted the conspiracy pushing AI more than the truthful one.

shows: A Ukrainian drone maker confirmed on record in June 2026 that ten quadcopters sent out in Terminator mode hunted down and killed two Russian soldiers with no human involved at any point.

shows: A 150 dollar strap on AI module lets cheap Ukrainian attack drones lock onto targets from 500 meters, aim for weak spots on vehicles, and keep hunting through jamming with a claimed hit rate above 90 percent.
